title: Hallway / Corridor Parameter Manifest
description: Six knobs an LLM front-end may turn for hallway.

ceiling_height_m.description: Floor-to-ceiling clear height of the corridor.
ceiling_height_m.rationale: Ceiling height is the dominant spatial variable in hallways. Vartanian et al. (2015) show ceiling height between 2.4-3.0m significantly modulates approach-avoidance decisions in circulation spaces.

corridor_width_m.description: Clear width of the corridor between finished wall surfaces.
corridor_width_m.rationale: Corridor width jointly determines perceived enclosure with ceiling height. Widths below 1.1m produce strong enclosure effects. Joye (2007) identifies narrow corridors as high cognitive-load environments.

wall_material_id.description: Named material token for corridor wall surfaces.
wall_material_id.rationale: Wall material warmth affects perceived stress in transitional spaces. Ulrich (1991) identifies material warmth as a primary wellness variable in enclosed circulation paths.

daylight_intensity.description: Scalar on natural light from windows or borrowed light openings.
daylight_intensity.rationale: Daylight access reduces perceived confinement and supports restoration during transit. Kaplan & Kaplan (1989) identify prospect-refuge balance as critical in corridor design.

biophilia_count.description: Number of plant entities placed along the corridor length.
biophilia_count.rationale: Plant presence provides restorative micro-encounters during transit. Ulrich (1991) reports brief exposure to greenery in transitional spaces reduces stress markers.

wall_warmth_index.description: 0 = cool palette (whites, greys); 1 = warm palette (creams, wood tones).
wall_warmth_index.rationale: Colour temperature interacts with ceiling height on mood. Meyers-Levy & Zhu (2007) show warm palettes under lower ceilings can compensate for enclosure-driven avoidance responses.
